reasons: Chou can reach Pharsa from angles and displace her out of a safe casting position.
Pharsa loses much of her value when she is forced to cancel artillery positioning.

proof.summary: Pharsa wants to stand at long range during Feathered Air Strike; Chou can flank and force her out of position before she finishes the cast window.
worksBestWhen: Chou approaches from side vision instead of walking through the frontline.
Chou saves displacement for Pharsa's real ultimate position.
failureCases: Pharsa keeps enough distance and uses Wings by Wings before Chou reaches her.

reasons: Lancelot can dodge Pharsa's delayed artillery and reach her backline position.
His mobility makes it difficult for Pharsa to rely only on range.

proof.summary: Pharsa's ultimate threatens fixed areas, while Lancelot can reposition through the fight and punish her during or after the casting window.
worksBestWhen: Lancelot waits for Pharsa to commit ultimate before diving.
Minions, jungle units, or enemy heroes give Lancelot dash paths.
failureCases: Lancelot dives without dash resets or misses the entry path.
Pharsa's team peels with hard crowd control.

reasons: Saber can punish Pharsa when she is isolated behind her frontline.
Pharsa is vulnerable if her escape is already used or interrupted.

proof.summary: Pharsa relies on safe spacing and Wings by Wings escape; Saber can punish her after that mobility is unavailable.
worksBestWhen: Saber tracks Pharsa's Wings by Wings cooldown.
Saber attacks from fog or after Pharsa starts ultimate.
failureCases: Saber uses ultimate into defensive items or backup control.

reasons: Hayabusa can threaten Pharsa from side lanes or jungle angles.
His target access punishes Pharsa when she casts from predictable backline spots.

proof.summary: Pharsa wants a safe stationary casting zone; Hayabusa can pressure that zone if he has shadow access and Pharsa lacks immediate peel.
worksBestWhen: Hayabusa enters from flank vision, not straight front.
Pharsa is separated from tank and support.
failureCases: Pharsa groups tightly and Hayabusa's damage spreads poorly.
Hayabusa dives before Pharsa commits her escape.

reasons: Natalia can pressure Pharsa's backline positioning before fights start.
Pharsa is much weaker when forced to spend escape defensively before casting ultimate.

proof.summary: Pharsa wants to set up from a safe distance, while Natalia can force Pharsa to respect flank threat and delay her artillery timing.
worksBestWhen: Natalia hides on the expected rotation path before objectives.
Natalia waits until Pharsa is separated from peel.
failureCases: Pharsa's team keeps vision around her.
Natalia reveals too early and gets zoned out.
